User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Aulas Online Senra platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

photoUrl.description: URL of the user's profile photo.

role.description: Role of the user (e.g., admin, student, teacher).

Subject.title: Subject
Subject.description: Represents a subject or discipline offered on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the subject entity.

TeacherProfile.title: TeacherProfile
TeacherProfile.description: Represents the profile information for a teacher.

id.description: Unique identifier for the teacher profile entity.

description.description: Description of the teacher's background and expertise.

availability.description: Available time slots for the teacher.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lesson entity.

status.description: Status of the lesson (e.g., scheduled, completed, cancelled).

PaymentPackage.title: PaymentPackage
PaymentPackage.description: Represents a package of lessons that can be purchased.

id.description: Unique identifier for the payment package entity.

name.description: Name of the payment package (e.g., 4 lessons, 8 lessons).

numberOfLessons.description: Number of lessons included in the package.

PaymentTransaction.title: PaymentTransaction
PaymentTransaction.description: Represents a payment transaction made by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the payment transaction entity.

transactionDate.description: Date and time of the transaction.

paymentMethod.description: Payment method used (e.g., PayPal, Mercado Pago).

status.description: Status of the transaction (e.g., completed, pending, failed).

SessionFeedback.title: SessionFeedback
SessionFeedback.description: Represents feedback provided by a student after a lesson session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the session feedback entity.

rating.description: Rating given by the student (e.g., 1-5 stars).

comments.description: Additional comments provided by the student.

feedbackDate.description: Date and time when the feedback was submitted.

definition.description: Stores user profiles.  Accessible by the user and administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Documents in this collection grant admin privileges to the corresponding user ID. Existence of a document grants admin access.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the administrator user.

definition.description: Stores information about subjects offered on the platform.

params.name: subjectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the subject.

definition.description: Stores teacher profiles. Each profile contains information about the teacher, including the subjects they teach.

params.name: teacherProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the teacher profile.

definition.description: Stores lessons associated with a specific student. Includes studentId as part of the path to enforce ownership and authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lesson.

definition.description: Stores information about payment packages that students can purchase.

params.name: paymentPackageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the payment package.

definition.description: Stores payment transactions made by a user. Includes userId in the path to enforce ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: paymentTransactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the payment transaction.

definition.description: Stores session feedback for a specific lesson. While this could be nested under the user, keeping it separate allows listing all feedback more easily.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lesson.

params.name: sessionFeedbackId
params.description: The unique identifier of the session feedback.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the Aulas Online Senra application, focusing on user management, scheduling, payments, and feedback.  The structure emphasizes Authorization Independence by denormalizing data where necessary, particularly for collaborative data.  It is also structured to be very explicit, enhancing debuggability and maintainability. Segregation is used to keep documents of different authorization requirements separate.

Authorization Independence is primarily achieved through path-based ownership for user-specific data and the Membership Map pattern for collaborative scenarios (though no collaborative collections were explicitly requested).  For instance, lessons are stored under the student's user ID, ensuring only the student (and admins) can access them directly. Teacher profiles are stored under the teacher's user ID. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to verify ownership.

The structure supports the required QAPs (Rules are not Filters) through structural segregation. User-specific data is stored under their respective user IDs, which enables secure `list` operations. Admins require special handling; they are given explicit access via a global roles collection, `roles_admin`.  This allows listing all items within a collection. For collaborative features, a membership map (`members`) would be denormalized to support QAPs.

Explicit state modeling uses a `status` field for entities like `Lesson` and `PaymentTransaction` for clarity.  Predictable schema avoids dynamic keys and enforces consistent naming conventions, such as always using `userId` for user references. Wildcards are descriptive, like `{userId}` and `{lessonId}`.
